era_context: The Carolingian Empire under Louis the Pious is in its final years. The Abbasid Caliphate under al-Mu'tasim is consolidating power. The Byzantine Empire under Theophilus is preparing for military campaigns. The Tang Dynasty in China is in decline. Viking raids on Western Europe are intensifying. This period marks the transition from the early medieval period of Carolingian dominance to the fragmented medieval world of the 9th–10th centuries.

events.title: Frankish succession disputes intensify
events.description: The succession dispute between Louis the Pious's sons—Lothair I, Louis the German, and Charles the Bald—intensifies. Regional magnates are openly aligning with different sons, creating factional tensions throughout the empire. Louis the Pious attempts to maintain control, but his authority is visibly weakening. The dispute is exacerbated by the position of Judith of Bavaria, Louis's second wife and mother of Charles the Bald, who is actively promoting her son's interests. The year 836 marks a critical point in the breakdown of Carolingian central authority.
events.certainty_note: The succession disputes of the 830s are well-documented in Frankish annals. The specific year 836 is consistent with documented patterns of escalating tensions.
cross_references: Part of broader Carolingian succession crisis (830s–840s)
Leads to 840-001 (Louis the Pious's death and succession crisis)

events.title: Al-Mu'tasim's reign consolidates Abbasid power
events.description: Al-Mu'tasim, now three years into his reign (r. 833–842), has consolidated his authority over the Abbasid Caliphate. He is expanding the use of slave soldiers (ghulams) and building a new military elite. He is also engaged in theological disputes over the nature of the Qur'an (the Mihna, or inquisition). His reign represents a shift toward military autocracy and the militarization of the caliphate. Al-Mu'tasim is preparing for military campaigns against the Byzantine Empire.

sources.name: Tabari's History of the Prophets and Kings

events.certainty_note: Al-Mu'tasim's reign dates (833–842) are well-established in Islamic historiography. His policies and military reforms are documented in later chronicles based on contemporary records.
cross_references: Part of broader Abbasid military transformation (830s–840s)

events.title: Viking raids on Frankish territories continue
events.description: Norse raiders continue to conduct raids on Frankish coastal and river regions. The raids are part of the intensifying Viking expansion into Western Europe during the 830s–840s. The weakness of Frankish central authority makes coordinated defense difficult. The raids presage the eventual establishment of Viking settlements in Normandy and other regions.
events.certainty_note: Viking raids on Frankish territories are documented in contemporary annals for the 830s–840s. The specific year 836 is consistent with documented patterns, though exact dating of individual raids is sometimes approximate.
cross_references: Related to 837-003 (Viking raids on Frankish coast and rivers)

disconfirming_evidence: The notion that the Frankish succession crisis was primarily a political dispute between Louis's sons has been revised by modern scholarship. Some historians argue that the crisis was fundamentally driven by regional magnate interests and that the sons were largely tools of competing factions. Additionally, the significance of Judith of Bavaria in promoting Charles the Bald's interests has been debated; some scholars argue that her role was less decisive than contemporary chroniclers suggested. The crisis may have been less about personal ambition and more about structural changes in Frankish political organization.
historiographic_note: The year 836 is moderately well-documented for Western Europe and the Islamic world, but documentation is heavily weighted toward political and military events recorded by clerical chroniclers. The Frankish annals are our primary source for Western Europe, and they reflect the perspective of the ecclesiastical establishment. Islamic sources are later compilations. Sub-Saharan Africa, the Americas, and much of Asia remain undocumented for this year. The 'Western European perspective' dominates surviving documentation. The year 836 is part of a broader period of transition from the Carolingian order to the fragmented medieval world.

graph_edges.note: The succession disputes of 836 led directly to the civil war following Louis's death in 840
Al-Mu'tasim's consolidation of power in 836 enabled the Abbasid military campaigns of 838
Viking raids of 836 were part of the broader intensification of Norse attacks in the 830s–840s
